01_initial_request.input_data: Existing fitness landscape theory and empirical datasets from literature
01_initial_request.user_intent: To review the role of epistasis in adaptation and integrate evolutionary theory with empirical data
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: N/A (Review Article); Objective is conceptual integration and identification of future directions

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fitness landscape theory quantifies the relationship between genotype and fitness. [Gap] The specific impact of tunable epistasis on evolutionary trajectories needs theoretical characterization. [Decision] To achieve conceptual integration, chose to use Theoretical Model Analysis to perform evaluation of the Rough Mount Fuji (RMF) model on genotype-fitness mappings, expecting to obtain expectations on peak accessibility and trajectory constraints.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Epistasis introduces potential for parallel fitness evolution toward different adapted genotypes; increasing number of peaks decreases length of adaptive trajectories and makes evolution more repeatable.

references.relevance: Recovery of features of experimental fitness landscapes by RMF

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Phenotype-fitness models offer a complementary perspective to probabilistic models. [Gap] How epistasis arises from nonlinear phenotype-fitness maps needs clarification. [Decision] To achieve conceptual integration, chose to use Theoretical Model Analysis to perform evaluation of Fisher's Geometric Model (FGM) on phenotype-fitness mappings, expecting to obtain insights on distance-dependent epistasis.

parameters.selection_type: stabilizing selection for an optimum phenotype

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutations additive on phenotypic level can be epistatic on fitness level; strong epistasis occurs when mutations find themselves on nearby genetic backgrounds close to the phenotypic optimum.

references.relevance: FGM defined with explicit genotype-phenotype map

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Recent advances enable empirical assessment of fitness landscapes. [Gap] Prevalence of epistasis in controlled laboratory settings needs evaluation. [Decision] To achieve integration of theory and data, chose to use Empirical Data Synthesis to perform review of engineered fitness landscape studies on microbial genotypes, expecting to obtain evidence on epistasis frequency and trajectory accessibility.

parameters.study_systems: E. coli beta-lactamase, S. cerevisiae Hsp90
parameters.accessibility_finding: 18 out of 120 possible evolutionary trajectories accessible (Weinreich et al. 2006)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Epistasis is common; diminishing-returns epistasis is pervasive; evolutionary trajectory is constrained by epistasis; 85% of substitutions depending on genetic background (Pokusaeva et al. 2019).

references.relevance: Large dataset spanning four million genotypes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Epistasis footprint may exist in natural evolutionary trajectories. [Gap] Evidence for epistatic adaptive trajectories in the wild is limited. [Decision] To achieve integration of theory and data, chose to use Empirical Data Synthesis to perform review of wild evolutionary trajectory studies on viral and insect proteins, expecting to obtain evidence on contingency and hybrid incompatibility.

parameters.study_systems: Human influenza nucleoprotein, Monarch butterfly toxin resistance, Pupfish ecotypes
parameters.methods: reverse genetics, CRISPR-Cas9-based genome editing, field enclosures

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Strong positive epistasis for toxin tolerance found; order of substitutions constrained by epistasis; hybrid incompatibilities mapped revealing hundreds of pairwise incompatibilities.

references.relevance: Dissection of epistatic evolutionary trajectory of influenza NP
Convergent evolutionary trajectory of toxin resistance

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fitness landscapes may change across environments. [Gap] Knowledge on environment-dependent epistasis (GxGxE) is limited. [Decision] To achieve integration of theory and data, chose to use Empirical Data Synthesis to perform review of environment-dependent fitness landscape studies on microbial and insect populations, expecting to obtain evidence on landscape ruggedness variation.

parameters.study_systems: E. coli, S. cerevisiae, Stick insects, Stickleback
parameters.environments: eight different environments, antibiotic dose gradients, fungicide presence

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Fitness landscapes differ significantly across environments in terms of absolute effect sizes, location and number of peaks and valleys; epistasis is both strong and environment specific.

references.relevance: Environment changes epistasis to alter trade-offs
Predictable properties of fitness landscapes induced by adaptational tradeoffs
Multi-environment fitness landscapes of a tRNA gene

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ecological factors may smoothen the fitness landscape in the wild. [Gap] Open challenges exist in integrating ecological factors into fitness landscape theory. [Decision] To achieve identification of future directions, chose to use Conceptual Integration to perform synthesis of ecological and evolutionary dynamics on fitness landscapes, expecting to obtain a framework for future research.

parameters.focus_areas: diploid/recombining populations, changing environments, ecological interactions
parameters.theoretical_concepts: fitness seascape, holey fitness landscape, realized epistasis

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Genomes may carry information about ecological history; environment-dependent epistasis could leave a footprint in genomes; need for interdisciplinary research integrating molecular, systems, and ecology viewpoints.

references.relevance: Genotype carrying information about ecological history

trajectory_accessibility.interpretation: Only a fraction of paths are accessible due to epistasis (Weinreich et al. 2006)

background_dependence.interpretation: Substitutions depend on genetic background, indicating ubiquitous epistasis (Pokusaeva et al. 2019)

epistasis_prevalence.interpretation: Epistasis is frequent and depends on fitness measure, mutations, and environment

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The review successfully integrates evolutionary theory with empirical data, confirming that epistasis is prevalent, often environment-dependent, and constrains adaptation, thereby achieving the objective of conceptual integration and identification of future directions.
